name: Democrats.org
subject: Please don’t click away from this short message →
body:
---
Good morning, fellow Democrats ☀️ Please don’t click away from this brief message. We’d like to share a few updates with you: We’re getting closer and closer to the midterms — our chance to take back Congress and check Trump’s power. [**With that in mind, we’re asking you to contribute $7 to the DNC today to help us elect Democrats up and down the ballot.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025?amount=7&refcode=em_20251119_5332_fr&refcode2=5344_22930333_JWg_09&akid=5344%2E22930333%2EJWg_09)
In 2025, we held town halls in every single state, invested in every state Democratic Party, and supported organizing efforts in every corner of the country to mobilize voters everywhere. _And it worked._ We won or overperformed in key races all across the country this year, from the rubiest of red to the bluest of blue districts. If this year showed us anything, it’s that we have to show up everywhere to win anywhere.
**We’re carrying this strategy into the 2026 midterms.** As the organization best positioned to distribute resources where they’re needed, when they’re needed, we’re hard at work building those resources _now._ The earlier we can make strategic investments, the clearer our path to winning back the House and Senate and preventing another two years of Trump with no guardrails from Congress.
[**If this work matters to you, we’re asking you to pitch in $7 to the DNC today to help us organize and invest nationwide next year. If you’re undecided, please remember any contribution makes an impact when the stakes are so high and we have very real fundraising goals to meet.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025?amount=7&refcode=em_20251119_5332_fr&refcode2=5344_22930333_JWg_09&akid=5344%2E22930333%2EJWg_09) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20251119_5332_fr&refcode2=5344_22930333_JWg_09&akid=5344%2E22930333%2EJWg_09)**

# [Leadership Demands Moral Courage](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=179372150&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5MzcyMTUwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjM1NzIzNzIsImV4cCI6MTc2NjE2NDM3MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.85XXlXlBg8sTWQFVkakvimLnnfKRlplyGgxsrri9R_A)
### What H.R. McMaster can teach us about leading with integrity -- and why his framework anchors my 4th annual Army-Navy policy conference.
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| Nov 19
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=179372150&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5MzcyMTUwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2MzU3MjM3MiwiZXhwIjoxNzY2MTY0MzcyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.nO0_Mdm-IqU8yUB-7S37uqHlI35_nrt-7h5fEX3y3tI&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=179372150&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5MzcyMTUwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjM1NzIzNzIsImV4cCI6MTc2NjE2NDM3MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.85XXlXlBg8sTWQFVkakvimLnnfKRlplyGgxsrri9R_A&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=179372150&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5MzcyMTUwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjM1NzIzNzIsImV4cCI6MTc2NjE2NDM3MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.85XXlXlBg8sTWQFVkakvimLnnfKRlplyGgxsrri9R_A)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvcGF0cmlja211cnBoeXBhL3AvbGVhZGVyc2hpcC1kZW1hbmRzLW1vcmFsLWNvdXJhZ2U_.LGj65zQWyqIvel1U-jE9f6do5Gs95xfrSp2aojczo00?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/leadership-demands-moral-courage?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

This past Thursday, I had the honor of giving the keynote speech at Valley Forge Military College, sponsored by the [H.R. McMaster Center for National & Cyber Security](https://substack.com/redirect/dd11344e-5808-4195-85ae-83840c467378?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) speaker series. I've stood at many podiums in my life -- in uniform, in Congress, in classrooms, and now at the intersection of business and public service -- but this time felt different. Valley Forge has produced distinguished leaders like Generals McMaster, Mike Linnington (USO CEO), Gus Perna (Operation Warp Speed), and current Maryland Governor Wes Moore, who found the place so formative he still wears his Valley Forge graduation ring every day.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/97e894b3-f68a-4a60-aeb9-71b1e35c394a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
You can watch my speech [here](https://substack.com/redirect/d18d89a3-fb53-47f2-89bf-4329fc3cf3e9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[](https://substack.com/redirect/d18d89a3-fb53-47f2-89bf-4329fc3cf3e9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
If my work resonates with you, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..icokEw9HufIL084RMJUpZE0SiykkhL0_ypxHG7Uzd6E?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=179372150)
That night, I was gathered with future Army officers, faculty, and business leaders drawn by the lessons of H.R. McMaster, whose career has shaped how the United States thinks about military and civilian leadership.
When I was a young Army officer, before joining the faculty at West Point, I read McMaster's 1997 _New York Times_ best-seller _Dereliction of Duty._ His book influenced how senior officers think about moral courage and how future wars are taught in our academies and war colleges. Even those who never opened its cover have absorbed its lessons through the leaders who did.

While he's written several other books, his first one was the most formative for me and one that should be required reading for those in uniform holding the sacred responsibility of serving their country.

The book is notable for its scathing rebuke of military leaders during the Vietnam War who lacked the moral courage to speak truth to power - a moral failure that led to 58,200 American deaths, including my namesake Patrick E. Ward, who gave his life trying to save others.
When I came to deliver my speech, I was thinking about all of this.
McMaster's work also dovetails with one of Bobby Kennedy's most famous quotes: "Moral courage is a rarer commodity than bravery in battle or great intelligence. Yet it is the one essential, vital quality for those who seek to change a world that yields most painfully to change."
Simply put, McMaster represents a mindset that America desperately needs to recover -- having a moral spine _.

_ Most people probably first learned his name in 2017 when, just 24 days into the first Trump administration, he was tapped to replace Michael Flynn as National Security Advisor, who had been forced out after lying to the FBI about his secret communications with the Russian ambassador. But like so many people in the first Trump Administration, McMaster, too, was eventually shown the door after 13 months. The country needed a steady hand, yet he was forced out for the same reason he was trusted: he told the truth even when it was unwelcome.

He governed by discipline in an administration that too often ran on wild impulse. He insisted on defending alliances like NATO and pushed back against conspiracy thinking, arguing for a structured national-security process. His integrity and distinguished career made him an indispensable asset to our country and a source of friction within Trump's inner circle. He refused to bend his integrity to fit anyone's political needs. And for those reasons, he's respected across party lines.
As a nation, we could learn a lot from McMaster. We need to commit to service rooted in integrity and love of country.
These are not simply "military traits." They're leadership traits, desperately needed across every domain of American life.

H.R.'s moral courage is a through line for me as I follow the path of the servant leader. From teaching West Point cadets to deploying to Iraq with the 82nd Airborne to serving in Congress and later as the Under Secretary of the Army, I have carried his example of country first and courage always.

As I mentioned in my [speech](https://substack.com/redirect/d18d89a3-fb53-47f2-89bf-4329fc3cf3e9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), when I was in Congress, I introduced the Murphy Amendment that overturned Don't Ask, Don't Tell: a policy that kicked out 13,000 troops for no other reason than being gay. People had told me at the time that if I did this I might not win re-election. (I didn't.) And that is part of the problem with the culture in Washington. As a straight, devout Catholic, it might have seemed counterintuitive that I supported this bill. But I believe that Judgement Day is more important than Election Day. I believe that someday I will have to answer to a higher power who will know exactly what I did when I had a responsibility to lead. Standing up for your principles is more important than trying to hold on to political power.
These are the kinds of teachings that H.R. McMaster instills in his work, and it's the same mindset that anchors our [Army-Navy policy conference](https://substack.com/redirect/4c56efab-486d-465d-839f-bfaf008f64ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that I'm hosting for the fourth consecutive year.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/77c3a99d-f943-4333-a16e-feb32da24f92?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
For the last four years, the day before the Army-Navy game, we've brought together veteran leaders in business and policy to explore what it means to lead with discipline, integrity, and service. If you're a veteran looking to grow your network, be part of the dynamic conversations shaping America's next generation of leaders and **[register here.](https://substack.com/redirect/4c56efab-486d-465d-839f-bfaf008f64ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
This is not just a sports weekend. It's an intellectual gathering and networking opportunity for our veteran community..

Our speaking lineup includes:
* Senator Dave McCormick
* Representative Chris Deluzio (PA-17)
* Medal of Honor recipient Florent "Flo" Groberg
* Blake Hall, CEO of [ID.me](https://substack.com/redirect/d907f42e-9a56-4c4a-9891-bdbd4cda0ccb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* Craig Cummings and Kelly Perdew, Moonshots Capital
* Frank Finelli, Partner, The Carlyle Group
* David Kurtz, CEO of Professional Services, Hilco Global

More to be announced soon!

📅 December 12, 2025
🕒 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM EST
📍 Johns Hopkins Carey School of Business -- Baltimore, MD
**[Register Here](https://substack.com/redirect/4c56efab-486d-465d-839f-bfaf008f64ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# The Four Pillars of the 2025 Army-Navy Policy Conference
Here's a preview of some of the topics that will be discussed.
**1\. Finance | Strategic Capital Under Pressure**
Carlisle's Frank Finelli, Hilco Global's David Kurtz, and others will explore how financial leaders navigate ambiguity, asymmetry, and risk. Markets reward discipline, not impulse. Capital allocators must make decisions with incomplete information, competing incentives, and geopolitical variability.
**2\. Technology | Trust, Ethics & Digital Resilience**
From [ID.me](https://substack.com/redirect/d907f42e-9a56-4c4a-9891-bdbd4cda0ccb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s Blake Hall to D-Code's Megan Metzger, this panel centers on a challenge McMaster knows better than most: how do you secure systems in a world designed for speed, not safety?
Identity, cybersecurity, AI, data integrity -- these are not technical topics alone. They are strategic questions about who we trust, why we trust them, and what we risk when that trust is misplaced.

**3\. Entrepreneurship | Founders as Modern Warriors

** Veterans, operators, and risk-takers share one common trait: they step forward when the outcome is uncertain.
This panel, with voices from Hilco Global, Moonshot Capital Founders, VetTV and more, is about founders who build with moral velocity and have the willingness to take calculated risk, adapt under pressure, and rally teams around shared purpose.

**4\. Public Service | Duty Beyond the Uniform**
US Senator Dave McCormick, Rep. Chris Deluzio, and Medal of Honor Recipient Florent Groberg will discuss the gap between military service and civil society and applying principle without partisanship.

These are just some of the topics that will be discussed, along with many more questions.
How do we lead in a moment defined by uncertainty?
How do we preserve integrity in systems that buckle under pressure?
How do we build institutions that can withstand the challenges ahead?
These are the kinds of questions McMaster asked.
And now, they are the questions we must answer.

The measure of whether our leaders are worthy of the responsibility they hold depends on the integrity they stand by.

I hope you'll join us!
-- Patrick

clean_body: What H.R. McMaster can teach us about leading with integrity -- and why his framework anchors my 4th annual Army-Navy policy conference. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Thank you for supporting my work. If my writing resonates with you, consider . Annual paid subscribers in the U.S. will receive a signed copy of my 2008 memoir, (shipping not available to Alaska, Hawaii, and U.S. territories). What H.R. McMaster can teach us about leading with integrity -- and why his framework anchors my 4th annual Army-Navy policy conference. | | | Nov 19 | | | | | | | | | | | | This past Thursday, I had the honor of giving the keynote speech at Valley Forge Military College, sponsored by the speaker series. I've stood at many podiums in my life -- in uniform, in Congress, in classrooms, and now at the intersection of business and public service -- but this time felt different. Valley Forge has produced distinguished leaders like Generals McMaster, Mike Linnington (USO CEO), Gus Perna (Operation Warp Speed), and current Maryland Governor Wes Moore, who found the place so formative he still wears his Valley Forge graduation ring every day. | | ---|---|--- You can watch my speech . If my work resonates with you, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. That night, I was gathered with future Army officers, faculty, and business leaders drawn by the lessons of H.R. McMaster, whose career has shaped how the United States thinks about military and civilian leadership. When I was a young Army officer, before joining the faculty at West Point, I read McMaster's 1997 New York Times best-seller Dereliction of Duty. His book influenced how senior officers think about moral courage and how future wars are taught in our academies and war colleges. Even those who never opened its cover have absorbed its lessons through the leaders who did. While he's written several other books, his first one was the most formative for me and one that should be required reading for those in uniform holding the sacred responsibility of serving their country. The book is notable for its scathing rebuke of military leaders during the Vietnam War who lacked the moral courage to speak truth to power - a moral failure that led to 58,200 American deaths, including my namesake Patrick E. Ward, who gave his life trying to save others. When I came to deliver my speech, I was thinking about all of this. McMaster's work also dovetails with one of Bobby Kennedy's most famous quotes: "Moral courage is a rarer commodity than bravery in battle or great intelligence. Yet it is the one essential, vital quality for those who seek to change a world that yields most painfully to change." Simply put, McMaster represents a mindset that America desperately needs to recover -- having a moral spine . Most people probably first learned his name in 2017 when, just 24 days into the first Trump administration, he was tapped to replace Michael Flynn as National Security Advisor, who had been forced out after lying to the FBI about his secret communications with the Russian ambassador. But like so many people in the first Trump Administration, McMaster, too, was eventually shown the door after 13 months. The country needed a steady hand, yet he was forced out for the same reason he was trusted: he told the truth even when it was unwelcome. He governed by discipline in an administration that too often ran on wild impulse. He insisted on defending alliances like NATO and pushed back against conspiracy thinking, arguing for a structured national-security process. His integrity and distinguished career made him an indispensable asset to our country and a source of friction within Trump's inner circle. He refused to bend his integrity to fit anyone's political needs. And for those reasons, he's respected across party lines. As a nation, we could learn a lot from McMaster. We need to commit to service rooted in integrity and love of country. These are not simply "military traits." They're leadership traits, desperately needed across every domain of American life. H.R.'s moral courage is a through line for me as I follow the path of the servant leader. From teaching West Point cadets to deploying to Iraq with the 82nd Airborne to serving in Congress and later as the Under Secretary of the Army, I have carried his example of country first and courage always. As I mentioned in my , when I was in Congress, I introduced the Murphy Amendment that overturned Don't Ask, Don't Tell: a policy that kicked out 13,000 troops for no other reason than being gay. People had told me at the time that if I did this I might not win re-election. (I didn't.) And that is part of the problem with the culture in Washington. As a straight, devout Catholic, it might have seemed counterintuitive that I supported this bill. But I believe that Judgement Day is more important than Election Day. I believe that someday I will have to answer to a higher power who will know exactly what I did when I had a responsibility to lead. Standing up for your principles is more important than trying to hold on to political power. These are the kinds of teachings that H.R. McMaster instills in his work, and it's the same mindset that anchors our that I'm hosting for the fourth consecutive year. | | ---|---|--- For the last four years, the day before the Army-Navy game, we've brought together veteran leaders in business and policy to explore what it means to lead with discipline, integrity, and service. If you're a veteran looking to grow your network, be part of the dynamic conversations shaping America's next generation of leaders and This is not just a sports weekend. It's an intellectual gathering and networking opportunity for our veteran community.. Our speaking lineup includes: Senator Dave McCormick Representative Chris Deluzio (PA-17) Medal of Honor recipient Florent "Flo" Groberg Blake Hall, CEO of Craig Cummings and Kelly Perdew, Moonshots Capital Frank Finelli, Partner, The Carlyle Group David Kurtz, CEO of Professional Services, Hilco Global More to be announced soon! 📅 December 12, 2025 🕒 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM EST 📍 Johns Hopkins Carey School of Business -- Baltimore, MD The Four Pillars of the 2025 Army-Navy Policy Conference Here's a preview of some of the topics that will be discussed. 1\. Finance | Strategic Capital Under Pressure Carlisle's Frank Finelli, Hilco Global's David Kurtz, and others will explore how financial leaders navigate ambiguity, asymmetry, and risk. Markets reward discipline, not impulse. Capital allocators must make decisions with incomplete information, competing incentives, and geopolitical variability. 2\. Technology | Trust, Ethics & Digital Resilience From Blake Hall to D-Code's Megan Metzger, this panel centers on a challenge McMaster knows better than most: how do you secure systems in a world designed for speed, not safety? Identity, cybersecurity, AI, data integrity -- these are not technical topics alone. They are strategic questions about who we trust, why we trust them, and what we risk when that trust is misplaced. 3\. Entrepreneurship | Founders as Modern Warriors Veterans, operators, and risk-takers share one common trait: they step forward when the outcome is uncertain. This panel, with voices from Hilco Global, Moonshot Capital Founders, VetTV and more, is about founders who build with moral velocity and have the willingness to take calculated risk, adapt under pressure, and rally teams around shared purpose. 4\. Public Service | Duty Beyond the Uniform US Senator Dave McCormick, Rep. Chris Deluzio, and Medal of Honor Recipient Florent Groberg will discuss the gap between military service and civil society and applying principle without partisanship. These are just some of the topics that will be discussed, along with many more questions. How do we lead in a moment defined by uncertainty? How do we preserve integrity in systems that buckle under pressure? How do we build institutions that can withstand the challenges ahead? These are the kinds of questions McMaster asked. And now, they are the questions we must answer. The measure of whether our leaders are worthy of the responsibility they hold depends on the integrity they stand by. I hope you'll join us! -- Patrick The Secretary's SitRep is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: I want to hear from you directly
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=1017%2E181023%2EOTbVOG)
Team,
Unlike my opponents in this race, I'm not being bought and sold by special interest dollars. I'm running to be a leader for YOU, and that means hearing from YOU about what matters to your family.
My team and I have been all over Michigan this year listening to voters. Every single person I talked to mentioned how it's getting harder and harder to get by in Michigan. Far too many people are being stretched too thin and have to work multiple jobs to just eke by.
We want a Michigan where people can thrive, where you can afford to do more than just live, but also grow and ensure your family is happy, healthy, and safe. We want to hear from you directly: What’s on your mind? What's keeping you up at night? What are your priorities for Michigan?
I’m in this to make our government work better for people, and it’s also why I’m asking you: **[Will you take a minute to fill out this short survey? Your responses are an important part of shaping our future in Michigan, so I hope you’ll make your voice heard today.](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/survey/2511-Priorities-survey?refcode=AU_JMB_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20251119_AM1_V1_S1&t=2&akid=1017%2E181023%2EOTbVOG)**
[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/survey/2511-Priorities-survey?refcode=AU_JMB_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20251119_AM1_V1_S1&t=3&akid=1017%2E181023%2EOTbVOG)
---
Thank you,
Jocelyn

* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with supporters like you.
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-jbmi/?t=4&akid=1017%2E181023%2EOTbVOG)

Ferndale, MI 48220

name: Ximena, United We Dream
subject: We are Powered by the People
body:
We’re building a future where everyone is free to live, speak, and be themselves. | | | Peter — you’re probably already getting emails about Black Friday. Well, here’s ours: a reminder that **our new merch store is available for all your gift-giving needs! 🧡** **The best part? Every purchase supports United We Dream!** [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.fcIMuZHs9Cen3hwX07DalxC-5UCEyM6KhWg857anj_fzsvEM7J6THGe6wn1jzeOndwi_hbWY_PD7lRXrUq7pVZiYEuGHrD2NPAitfkkY5NnudtJUb9kPSOuxK9fPiFB-fyZzpFjMDetGUOCUNLTIQfYIkepkZBzm1dsrparbjgfFaXj5qW6ErefDDmSQxJMsNz_mxK13F8L4bBhzSMEiZxE55vaUSHS71CFxKvb19tFOxOcy_exmzOmRopE1Nk5s4960OZGNcL5v5-tf-Tys1kTZvjAAHtgG5w5N2tJenjF3ADEw0EoAZozrnuNzVErvsNYii3w8v5FlaAESJXpSXQ/4lq/gQhjS2BQRWCEo8gNlpVaLw/h0/h001.6DWoKy6cdLBdnKpVHno1q7nHxNTSMFcFXXDUm5VuK7c) | | [SHOP MERCH](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.fcIMuZHs9Cen3hwX07DalxC-5UCEyM6KhWg857anj_fzsvEM7J6THGe6wn1jzeOndwi_hbWY_PD7lRXrUq7pVZiYEuGHrD2NPAitfkkY5NkrbTt2misaVtOALhFs_4aJPtkKPMoPM9YQxmuKpn9M4lX1b6gvC1QBYTaKFKofLyTkaG7JRBbby_dPFUo9HjaYrVO2gjR3aW40iUIrPl_tfJVPL9_qD3SuNHdg7065Lh_Kr_YAiELSQTxNa2h5xN21P3457TqA_IF1BrkRZMA_3yzNgcSbxckmNWdNJOG31gzNAGM8JaIakiNNmXlrBPbH5Lyp6P5jfmMjrWBALnSVEQ/4lq/gQhjS2BQRWCEo8gNlpVaLw/h1/h001.fRA5gzfa9YFaFmJ5GURy7mKtd88ohVIZO3TwHxGzR54)
---
Powered by the People is the simple idea that inside of all of us exists strength, wisdom, and economic power.
“We the People” means _all_ of us: our neighbors, our cousins, our friends, our co-workers, and our communities.
Everyone we have daily interactions with — people we see at the park, the grocery store, at our schools, and coffee shops — is part of the future we’re building together:**a future where everyone is free to live, speak, and be themselves**.
Our new streetwear partnership with The South celebrates culture, community, and collective power. By repping Powered by the People, you can help amplify our message far and wide and bring more people into our movement.
**And when we say Powered by the People, we mean it.** Our freedom, our art, and our movements are all connected. ✊🏽 None of us are free until all of us are free. 🧡 That’s the message we’re sending with this streetwear line.

clean_body: We’re building a future where everyone is free to live, speak, and be themselves. | | | Peter — you’re probably already getting emails about Black Friday. Well, here’s ours: a reminder that our new merch store is available for all your gift-giving needs! 🧡 The best part? Every purchase supports United We Dream! | | Powered by the People is the simple idea that inside of all of us exists strength, wisdom, and economic power. “We the People” means all of us: our neighbors, our cousins, our friends, our co-workers, and our communities. Everyone we have daily interactions with — people we see at the park, the grocery store, at our schools, and coffee shops — is part of the future we’re building together:a future where everyone is free to live, speak, and be themselves. Our new streetwear partnership with The South celebrates culture, community, and collective power. By repping Powered by the People, you can help amplify our message far and wide and bring more people into our movement. And when we say Powered by the People, we mean it. Our freedom, our art, and our movements are all connected. ✊🏽 None of us are free until all of us are free. 🧡 That’s the message we’re sending with this streetwear line. By sharing and wearing your United We Dream pride, you can join this work and help us support immigrant youth! | We are: Thank you, Ximena, United We Dream Follow us on: | | | | |

name: Star Parker
subject: A new kind of leadership for California
body:
I’d like to tell you a bit about myself
---
[View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e18825048/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvVVZ3WWJRSlRyRg/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMTk/LbmNsUnZQU2xZZw/qP2hpZHM9bmM4YTZTMUI1STRFWjVscw/gaR39Bw/s3t4d35775a)
| | | | | | | Star Parker [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
Patriot,

**My name is Star Parker, and I’m running to represent California’s 49th Congressional District.**

For over 30 years, I’ve called San Clemente home: the “Spanish Village by the Sea.” I’ve spent my life fighting for what matters most: **faith, freedom, and opportunity for all.**

Now, I’m stepping up to serve because I see our community — and our country — at a crossroads.
[Show Your Support >>](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) Washington has grown too big, too intrusive, and too disconnected from the families and small businesses that make America strong. The federal government is taking too much, controlling too much, and giving too little back to the people who actually make this nation work.
I know firsthand how such overreach hurts hardworking Americans. It’s time to take a stand, and it’s time for leaders who aren’t afraid to speak the truth.
**_That’s why I’m running._**

**To limit government, restore local control, and protect the freedoms that make America exceptional.**

[Show Your Support >>](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) But to build a movement rooted in courage and conviction, I need your help, Patriot. Your first donation today will send a powerful message: **that Californians are ready for bold, principled leadership again.** _**[Will you be one of the first to join my campaign with a gift of $10, $25, $50, or more today?>>](https://link.email.america1stwarroom. "Will you be one of the first to join my campaign with a gift of $10, $25, $50, or more today? >>")**_ [Show Your Support >>](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) Together, we can reshape the government and restore power to the people...where it belongs.

With faith and gratitude,
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
Star Parker
Candidate for Congress, CA-49

---

This email was sent to **[dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)** as part of your subscription to **America First War Room**

108 S Washington St. Suite 200
Alexandria, VA 22314

[Unsubscribe](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e18825048/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3N1YnNjcmlwdGlvbnMvdW5zdWJzY3JpYmU/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMTk/LbmNsUnZQU2xZZw/qP2hpZHM9bmM4YTZTMUI1STRFWjVscw/gaR39Bw/scj8e52553d)

The outpouring of grassroots support I’ve received since officially announcing my gubernatorial campaign has been truly incredible.

**Within just a few hours of my announcement, we raised over** _$1 MILLION_** for my gubernatorial campaign and the New York GOP! **

Those kinds of numbers will send a shiver down Kathy Hochul’s spine.

The worst governor in America knows she can’t win a free and fair election that’s decided on the issues. Her only hope is to BUY her way to reelection – so if we can keep this momentum going, she won’t stand a chance!

But that last part is key, Friend...

_**_To compete with the Democrats’ multimillion-dollar ad buys and win this race, we have to keep our momentum going from today all the way through Election Day._**_

clean_body: If we keep this up, she doesn’t stand a chance... | Friend, The outpouring of grassroots support I’ve received since officially announcing my gubernatorial campaign has been truly incredible. Within just a few hours of my announcement, we raised over $1 MILLION for my gubernatorial campaign and the New York GOP! Those kinds of numbers will send a shiver down Kathy Hochul’s spine. The worst governor in America knows she can’t win a free and fair election that’s decided on the issues. Her only hope is to BUY her way to reelection – so if we can keep this momentum going, she won’t stand a chance! But that last part is key, Friend... To compete with the Democrats’ multimillion-dollar ad buys and win this race, we have to keep our momentum going from today all the way through Election Day. That’s why I set an ambitious goal of raising an additional $1,000,000 by midnight tomorrow to ensure we close out our launch week in the strongest possible position. Let’s CRUSH this goal and show Hochul that her days in office are numbered! Thank you, Elise Stefanik Paid for by Elise for Governor PO Box 500 Glens Falls, NY 12801

subject: REVEALED: Bone-Chilling Discovery Stuns Rust Belt Town
body:

---
| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Something deeply troubling is unfolding in the Rust Belt’s forgotten towns—and now it’s all on camera.

Locals quietly share stories of events so strange, many once chose to look away—until this footage forced the truth into the open.

This chilling video documents what others refused to acknowledge.

I spoke with a witness whose experience is truly unforgettable.

If you think you know what’s happening in these places, prepare to see them in a whole new light.

[Click to watch the footage—see it before it disappears.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=094900d2c5694d0ca1659b79c5bbe04d&_e=HPSI2mOYpLMRyEkdGSZTJw-g1e_EiR6c2wIVUfZStkGD-PdqikeXFExQhI_AJYOTSyGJxJ3mUPTrE3ymAldlqZjqASYx2qBj-rrGpejVEpO8NvZc5WID0IrnKUEN-VPGYOy6d-TxKQvDaulmRJNqCeCGXsGM5y8yjbbhOr85exEmiCsUCWYstrGIlBfcRYr9kDYoboQblgRRqvqSwiAYA3Swt9DXAI7LrEgcnS5DcQL2_JhOpd6WjdstXKBWfXWwHkB5xD29Ot2Peh78jcV6lb3HXgfPMy1WogYd-2E8Luku6c3SsqoJBPlhf0fn-X2IHdeoAf8ze3_DX6aw7MXPUxN0RJpYvuMXkYlyMwMl3ZoZQ7vPY2yU1wgDZY8Qn9to75uky1alFkFsJpQtV3bayDUUqvx1a_ZjtAs_0hAGL5SHzI_Wfyntbthxz2mva2NE3nUUU9Y9Y4OEVGndRomTtGEqSvFDlA4jJt2kinVufK5eVHJvyEe-7EoSG_Pcy3-56y3drqUPYE4Ob3XDVybMIDhzF4fym_r3tEyN4i0iAqasVAYx5_U7e6H5reBCkqHc3qffPHiNQ2LpTLG5mOTMuw%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: Something deeply troubling is unfolding in the Rust Belt’s forgotten towns—and now it’s all on camera.| | | | November 19 | | | | | Best Card Exposed by Gas Pump Camera | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. clean_body: = Team: A few years ago we reached out to help our friends at the Tennessee Democratic County Chairs’ Association. NOW we have a huge opportunity to flip a ruby-red Congressional district that voted for Trump by 20 points. Democrat Aftyn Behn is running an incredibly competitive race right now in a special election in TN-07 – and it’s looking increasingly likely that we could flip this seat in Trump country, especially after the results of Election Day 2025. Democrats are overperforming and winning in places we’ve never competed in before, and we want to do everything in our power to push for a blue majority. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. = = = = = Democrat, everywhere matters. Fellow Patriot, Charlie Kirk knew something long before the rest of America caught on. That the next generation of young Americans were desperate for** ** truth... That the Left's grip on the culture wasn't as strong as it looked... And with boldness, hard work, and a focused strategy -- WE COULD WIN. Charlie didn't just talk about it. _He built a movement that's changed history._ **And long before he left us... he wrote _How We Win_**** -- the playbook that lays it all out.**
---
[ ](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=f9066a155e1342b6a5535d07ce835bd8&_e=cHIWdUTX4cnN4pE5iqYCHjA5oSgbyPrGmGXl6no_5Xi56O600mJAxp9CWEHPrJyYih5VLYnQYfIeTLX7ZIF133r-mDlc9Zs8BZjGTczotSEFdY8AMDBCijpVWbSu_dcuz4c5E2JcTlex2xwL3HcBSRPLRoSGwrYR1WqEt_nUAFH2hJqGEw4ciO8rnRIzcjRpydDlKqe73nrtpA1aApOQHYQFF8BVgRopc4nxuTcIRJXiSVADQi2Wmp0zKR1rQgJ7vJD6-ylvZPndUcIRuB9yrzcBxSig3GJhG3Z00WRzLHRcbBTmAmp6iYxRMEhydjAQi2GmNJ7UXNp_0H4yrYfeXVv1pbocZBgtTv8FQEG5GuAPp-CVbQ9q7AnndqHqS1b0CciVO_Z2YhF5vyfilauCILQtH0fMETBjbcLdnFZjQSwZlIW5mjnROIG7iFh-rahce3miTV2SdFAmSkYRwcrBZ9LICv6705HAETVLqOGEKg7KMpydZSmMmKWBrKus5xZoOAJ3VUQJPHJwpDZRBHY9BMlwW4Xma2pzsGh2D6MoGFrI4zrT7kM7UqrKpBxML9xEyAqC6MVAmhXXBiwPQl3-p0fZ1hznr_OO2k4z2-itaPm1AElXAYWTdz48nXDwlCHpYSGBwAvCKOgmbsnQM0siweHkCvSlps71NL9hyRNLarDWrfimGd9gG3X7ZgE2aY7IsU5tP6_Kisg4jJLQO8f-Qw%3D%3D)
---
Today, Turning Point USA wants to send it to you for FREE when you make a gift of any amount to carry Charlie's mission forward.
---
[ **Make My Gift &**
**Claim My FREE Book** ](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=f9066a155e1342b6a5535d07ce835bd8&_e=cHIWdUTX4cnN4pE5iqYCHjA5oSgbyPrGmGXl6no_5Xi56O600mJAxp9CWEHPrJyYih5VLYnQYfIeTLX7ZIF133r-mDlc9Zs8BZjGTczotSEFdY8AMDBCijpVWbSu_dcuz4c5E2JcTlex2xwL3HcBSRPLRoSGwrYR1WqEt_nUAFH2hJqGEw4ciO8rnRIzcjRpydDlKqe73nrtpA1aApOQHYQFF8BVgRopc4nxuTcIRJXiSVADQi2Wmp0zKR1rQgJ7d6l4e0SilfeBkMkgOFsczRza2CijfMjh0g4DCRjthuFKC9wZw3tXdo0ujJcMy4v8iM17pTfuNnOwmX5MLTX8NOpsKLhdt1m3kCaVgo0W04IAv9sMhwJfQk3sGblk6bH30Dj03l4lCNMFFP5MyfDltDdhxxTfA0CZRwXsi9Jqoz3RMYSLdHCFdcp5pdmrdFDOAJCYI_m0w5ePG6yWOCYg2aDRqrSbIwW--aD30SNsdwdj8qLsq-a4zPEqL5N-fhwIy9xM4ZhSe0XZuL9nfWCc6f6wR91wIDPjD50OdDnaKWLESiujeqUdWi1GyKyidZkqA17R-GYwf9ga3WcJqXZ_v4xA8qgL40jhhXPfdmiNaK6CSWp1_Blb1DFjzvZrVDHUyBtvJa12DpeyYj4zo8k1AcGSafbGPxKt1X8sL3gGeTuUtJV1UrA2WxEv3SKJUY-dMWTPbFXdymbM4wvVDjNJcg%3D%3D)
---
Fellow Patriot, this isn't just a book... **It 's the exact blueprint Charlie used to:** 🔥 Launch Turning Point USA chapters in all 50 states

body:
If Democrats get their way, they’ll sabotage our vision for America

Friend,
America is standing at a dangerous crossroads. The socialist Left is tightening its grip -- and now, they've set their sights on Tennessee.
A [Special Election](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2ztkf31ro7mp7t9b67karmi0f4t3isra988n6qpkg_rp22sh2s8i6eor6clie8ob1c9h2bs) has just been announced in Tennessee's [7th Congressional District](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zb83dlop9o44r5ume95c8bmonaf04n1r3g5g65h0_rp22sh2s8i6eor6clie8ob1c9h2bs), and this could be our last chance to stop the radical Left from advancing their socialist agenda.
[Proven conservative Matt Van Epps](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2ztcjb3pn98lgf4eoanjurkfjqqdf5ecb4sv2mbhg_rp22sh2s8i6eor6clie8ob1c9h2bs) is stepping up to defend our values -- faith, family, and freedom -- against [radical liberal Aftyn Behn](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zc30o3evph51gk1ujkhh017gcnr47fg4lljcqdno_rp22sh2s8i6eor6clie8ob1c9h2bs), a puppet of the socialist machine determined to make '[God's Country' look like New York City.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z68g5vbqt7jfeo0blfen83rfkmjdtiejhnfncpgg_rp22sh2s8i6eor6clie8ob1c9h2bs)
But here's the terrifying truth:
\- The liberal elite are pouring millions into this race.
\- They're determined to flip this seat -- and take one more step toward total socialist control.
[If we don't act RIGHT NOW, they might win.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zaa7g0h7l5i6engir7tq4tn50120ufipb0vcbp8o_rp22sh2s8i6eor6clie8ob1c9h2bs)
We must match their funding dollar-for-dollar and rally every conservative in America to fight back.
America's future is on the line -- [will you stand up before it's too late?](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zdefr0ger5gsm3lnhv0dvp5r2ampo6m9rhl0r3qo_rp22sh2s8i6eor6clie8ob1c9h2bs)[🚨 JOIN THE FIGHT & DEFEND AMERICA 🚨

Peter, we're asking you to remember how you felt when you learned that Abigail Spanberger would be Virginia's next Governor.
Remember when you saw the margin was fifteen points.
Remember when you realized that all of the time, energy, and money we had invested had paid off.
**Really remember it — and hold onto that feeling.**
Peter, we can't allow that feeling to go away.
We need to build on it, to channel the excitement into momentum heading into Abigail's term as Governor and into the next set of elections for Governor and for Congress in less than a year's time.

name: Ileana Paules Bronet
subject: “Quiet, piggy.”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Her Bold Move Action
body:
You kind of have to watch the video to see how... | | |
---
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119584182/583572078/-1509058847?refcode=EM_F_251119Quiet_TH_7_90DO_G_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImYyNDdmOWMwLTZmYzUtZjAxMS04MTk2LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Fz6sj6gQdQ8vY5J_Dufi2hbDn_FfDFj6WiwaVNK1OeY=)
---
Peter,

This week, Congress voted to call on the Justice Department to release the Epstein files. Trump, who has blatant ties to Epstein and appears to be guilty of the absolute worst claims leveled at him, initially stood in opposition to the files being released. However, after he realized he was losing control of his coalition, he reversed his stance.

As if women haven’t had a chilling enough week at the hands of Donald Trump and too many powerful men to count, an unsettling video has been circulating in which a _female_ _reporter_ tried to ask President Trump a follow-up question regarding the release of the Epstein files. He responds by pointing at her and shouting, **“Quiet! Quiet, piggy.”**

**Peter,[_when you watch this video, it’s hard not to see the violence in Trump’s eyes. This is, without a doubt, a chilling video._ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119584183/583572079/-1509058847?refcode=EM_F_251119Quiet_TH_7_90DO_G_N_L1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImYyNDdmOWMwLTZmYzUtZjAxMS04MTk2LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Fz6sj6gQdQ8vY5J_Dufi2hbDn_FfDFj6WiwaVNK1OeY=)** We refuse to accept or normalize this behavior – **and we are working tirelessly to block men like this from getting elected to the halls of power across the country.** Enough is enough.
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119584184/583572080/-1509058847?refcode=EM_F_251119Quiet_TH_7_90DO_G_N_P1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImYyNDdmOWMwLTZmYzUtZjAxMS04MTk2LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Fz6sj6gQdQ8vY5J_Dufi2hbDn_FfDFj6WiwaVNK1OeY=)
---
We have seen the way men in power use that power to demean and harm women. From the way Trump spoke to that reporter, to the thousands of examples from the Epstein emails. **Here’s the good news:** We know the perfect way to combat the misogyny, the abuses of power, the boys’ clubs that exclude and objectify women, and, all too often, the real-world violence against women. WE ARE COMMITTED TO ELECTING **MORE WOMEN** TO THE HALLS OF POWER. To the rooms where decisions about our lives, our safety, and our bodies are being made.

**Unfortunately, we’ve spent the past week gaining intimate knowledge about how powerful men use that power against women. If we wouldn’t want to be left alone with these men in a room, why would we ever trust them to be the ones making laws about our bodies, our safety, and our wellbeing?

We need more women in government. Period. **

[**Help support our work to elect qualified, brave, compassionate women who will fight to defend the women of America in every possible way by rushing $20.26 or more today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119584185/583572081/-1509058847?refcode=EM_F_251119Quiet_TH_7_90DO_G_N_L2&amount=20.26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImYyNDdmOWMwLTZmYzUtZjAxMS04MTk2LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Fz6sj6gQdQ8vY5J_Dufi2hbDn_FfDFj6WiwaVNK1OeY=)

clean_body: You kind of have to watch the video to see how... | | | | | = Peter, This week, Congress voted to call on the Justice Department to release the Epstein files. Trump, who has blatant ties to Epstein and appears to be guilty of the absolute worst claims leveled at him, initially stood in opposition to the files being released. However, after he realized he was losing control of his coalition, he reversed his stance. As if women haven’t had a chilling enough week at the hands of Donald Trump and too many powerful men to count, an unsettling video has been circulating in which a female reporter tried to ask President Trump a follow-up question regarding the release of the Epstein files. He responds by pointing at her and shouting, “Quiet! Quiet, piggy.” Peter,= We refuse to accept or normalize this behavior – and we are working tirelessly to block men like this from getting elected to the halls of power across the country. Enough is enough. = We have seen the way men in power use that power to demean and harm women. From the way Trump spoke to that reporter, to the thousands of examples from the Epstein emails. Here’s the good news: We know the perfect way to combat the misogyny, the abuses of power, the boys’ clubs that exclude and objectify women, and, all too often, the real-world violence against women. WE ARE COMMITTED TO ELECTING MORE WOMEN TO THE HALLS OF POWER. To the rooms where decisions about our lives, our safety, and our bodies are being made. Unfortunately, we’ve spent the past week gaining intimate knowledge about how powerful men use that power against women. If we wouldn’t want to be left alone with these men in a room, why would we ever trust them to be the ones making laws about our bodies, our safety, and our wellbeing? Fellow Conservative,

Last week, Bernie Sanders—the self-proclaimed democratic socialist—threw his full weight behind my opponent in the United States Senate contest in which we’re candidates.

In his endorsement of Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan, Sanders called her the fighter we need to “ _**stand up for working people against the billionaires and corporate interests.**_ ”

**Translation:** She’s his pick to push for socialized, government-run healthcare, $17 minimum wage, higher taxes on job creators, and overturning Citizens United to flood D.C. with more big-government cash.

That’s not leadership.

**That’s the socialism I fought against overseas.**

I’ve trained allied navies in the South China Sea.

_**I’m Adam Schwarze—Marine, SEAL, nuclear strategist, capitalist warrior. I’ve deterred real enemies to protect free enterprise. Now I’ll deter D.C. weakness and socialist overreach.**_

As a _**Navy SEAL Officer**_ , I deployed _**nine times**_ across 70+ countries—from three combat tours in Iraq as a Marine infantryman, to commanding a Crisis Alert Force in the Middle East, to humanitarian missions in Africa.

I’ve seen socialism crush economies firsthand: families starving under state control, initiative erased by endless mandates, freedom traded for false security.

That’s why I risked my life—to defend American capitalism, the engine that built the greatest nation on earth.

Now, I’m risking my career and professional reputation to run for the United States Senate in Minnesota. I’m the underdog, but Republicans have a very real shot at flipping this Senate seat for the first time in _**DECADES.**_

What I don’t have right now is money. _**[If you still believe capitalism is worth fighting for, would you please consider a small investment in my campaign today? $5 or even $10 will do if you can spare it.](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=6250844e4c734727a629422f447a6aa9&_e=eYFMl0F5AJqoCKZ_W1_jYIXorLTaWEAHPSHJx4GIKhzTstEH8GbClAtZpiPLQJMyvXPtCShOJpDMaqqfdFAZg2vm5HjAQu5SJdPpJZoqFPHvlUsg8TC1sjTbBH0dfR2rLky6hGCwAL4I20NDLNhMz_YXokmCi9oO-xen7-QiQcxOOahIPSkVI-pnImubbat46UuMkIuv9ZnDfPqruDnivceCKgMDmPIkka2MQON26rWEKplMK-5_Cgz4K0YbXOyFa7Nmr7t8b6JNcmd55r7E2lfuUgTKwD6OfPwEISckh9JtCXoI_JThBUK_9yFRba2ng3LeYQVkoaBwNW2yZlUCPNTZZZTovBbuUcUJb9I1yW4DGkeEhO88BIzzcMM6y6mxC1T2LO2lbz6xg-tWQQn7pJgJeUPeUOIFoyCU7LQC8y-hJvsKWeZBiKqCjB9nH9_V0VRAWq2FsfkWGeq3z_Z-5arqzEel0sc5IxjONo4ZR8UVXtWE-67w-c38j76bCgJKPkFn46P3_QOTtJRfMqavWxJdsGHV7ci7iP2WC4_TZtU4SmOBaafUg8drRam2DpZlBUUgNQ5rMQj0yQ6KzBH3CrjoCcl5FuK_pzXxKCO5iBn0xVDHn3RuZ-IB_DRW1q9H8_pr0hsT--keNCHERDnvGfymC7bzETFm4RIVgodTr6Gk5hzkNLv14GEHQf6UMX9jtHcLBb1UgWwlkubBCAfHmvL1MnuQdNGoUYR9mFlrReVbvFXXB8PKHYnhk1ClmxWhUvz4YCxH_Hu1F6qpjqFffA%3D%3D)**_

\- Now, with Bernie’s blessing, she’s eyeing the Senate to nationalize healthcare and tax away innovation.

President Trump is already restoring deterrence—tariffs on China, sealed borders, and energy independence.

He needs allies like me: a SEAL who’s deterred real enemies in war zones, not dreamed them up in committee rooms.

My opponent’s Bernie-backed vision? It’s the same failed playbook I’ve seen abroad—quietly obedient societies, where the human spirit withers.

clean_body: I’ve seen socialism crush economies firsthand: families starving under state control, initiative erased by endless mandates, freedom traded for false security. | Fellow Conservative, Last week, Bernie Sanders—the self-proclaimed democratic socialist—threw his full weight behind my opponent in the United States Senate contest in which we’re candidates. In his endorsement of Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan, Sanders called her the fighter we need to “ stand up for working people against the billionaires and corporate interests. ” Translation: She’s his pick to push for socialized, government-run healthcare, $17 minimum wage, higher taxes on job creators, and overturning Citizens United to flood D.C. with more big-government cash. That’s not leadership. That’s the socialism I fought against overseas. I’ve trained allied navies in the South China Sea. I’m Adam Schwarze—Marine, SEAL, nuclear strategist, capitalist warrior. I’ve deterred real enemies to protect free enterprise. Now I’ll deter D.C. weakness and socialist overreach. As a Navy SEAL Officer , I deployed nine times across 70+ countries—from three combat tours in Iraq as a Marine infantryman, to commanding a Crisis Alert Force in the Middle East, to humanitarian missions in Africa. I’ve seen socialism crush economies firsthand: families starving under state control, initiative erased by endless mandates, freedom traded for false security. That’s why I risked my life—to defend American capitalism, the engine that built the greatest nation on earth. Now, I’m risking my career and professional reputation to run for the United States Senate in Minnesota. I’m the underdog, but Republicans have a very real shot at flipping this Senate seat for the first time in DECADES. What I don’t have right now is money. My socialist opponent’s record? \- As Tim Walz’s right hand, she sued to defund ICE detention, letting illegals with records roam free—while fentanyl from cartel labs (fueled by open borders) kills 1,200 Minnesotans a year. \- Now, with Bernie’s blessing, she’s eyeing the Senate to nationalize healthcare and tax away innovation. President Trump is already restoring deterrence—tariffs on China, sealed borders, and energy independence. He needs allies like me: a SEAL who’s deterred real enemies in war zones, not dreamed them up in committee rooms. My opponent’s Bernie-backed vision? It’s the same failed playbook I’ve seen abroad—quietly obedient societies, where the human spirit withers. he left’s poll shows a Republican within 2 points of flipping this seat. We need FINANCIAL FIREPOWER to counter Bernie’s political machine, which is equipped with hundreds of millions of dollars and an email fundraising list 10 miles long. Every dollar arms the next generation of American strength—and will help us bury Bernie’s agenda in Minnesota. For a better tomorrow, Adam Schwarze Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota Former Navy SEAL officer Marine Corps Veteran Proud Minnesotan P.S: Deterrence works when your adversary believes you’ll act. China believes me. Bernie fears me. FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

name: Rick Larsen
subject: Increasing transparency in aviation
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Citizens to Elect Rick Larsen
body:
The administration’s lack of coordination with key aviation stakeholders raises real questions
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119584433/583572661/-201694504?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWY0ZjBiYi03MGM1LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WVG92Hm2PoBv17iVlT1z1HIMV0Kwm1FnUdvyh0s5hAU=&emci=6ab32c48-9bc1-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=daf4f0bb-70c5-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=9494239)
---

| | As the federal government reopens, I remain deeply concerned about the Trump Administration's decision to mandate a sudden 10 percent reduction in flight operations at 40 major airports. **While we all agree that aviation safety must be the top priority, the administration has yet to provide the necessary data or transparency to justify such an unprecedented move**. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119584434/583572662/274120667?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWY0ZjBiYi03MGM1LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WVG92Hm2PoBv17iVlT1z1HIMV0Kwm1FnUdvyh0s5hAU=&emci=6ab32c48-9bc1-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=daf4f0bb-70c5-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=9494239) **That is why I led 26 of my colleagues on the House Transportation and Infrastructure Committee in demanding answers from the Department of Transportation and the Federal Aviation Administration**. We are seeking:
* Clear information about the safety trends the FAA says it identified
* What specific risks exist at each airport affected
* How the administration coordinated with airlines and labor unions, and
* What criteria will guide the return to normal operations.

**The administration's lack of coordination with key aviation stakeholders raises real questions about whether this mandate was driven by genuine safety concerns or by political motives during the government shutdown.** My Senate colleagues, including Senators Murray and Schumer, have echoed concerns about the FAA's transparency and the way this decision was communicated to the public. Although the shutdown has ended and the FAA has paused the full 10 percent cut, airlines are still facing a 6 percent reduction at major hubs like Seattle-Tacoma International Airport. As the FAA evaluates next steps, Congress needs a full briefing to ensure this process is data-driven, transparent, and collaborative. **We all share the goal of maintaining the safest aviation system in the world. But any actions taken must be based on clear evidence and open communication, not abrupt directives issued without accountability.** More soon, _Rick_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Rick Larsen instantly:

name: Team Phil
subject: Sky-high health care costs can’t continue
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Phil Weiser for Colorado
body:
A constellation of health care ideas.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119585160/583575241/1239926708?refcode=em_WEI_FR_HealthCare_111925_B&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTA2ODU4ZS03MWM1LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xmxX3E_rTadXZxnm6M-pPf4Nu-gDgNrefLNQ5DSpksI=&emci=63907773-6fc5-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=4106858e-71c5-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=51844520) Friend, here in Colorado, our health care system is struggling. Coloradans who need care are paying too much, and access to care is too limited. Hospital systems, especially in rural Colorado, are at risk, and providers can be scarce. As governor, Phil will work collaboratively and creatively across state government and health care systems to navigate our state to better outcomes. Check out our constellation of ideas to bring high-quality, affordable health care to all Coloradans. name: Rudy Giuliani
subject: Regarding the President’s Pardon
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Legal Defense Trust
body:
I’m fighting until the final hour!
| [](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=3df60693c617404e944324494ba93650&_e=Zr9ANEmydXr9lLkTE1YMXs1wM75M5uiiiga3rP_lPEU72OLQm-fDM36UIs2GxD_Pr43aTvz-1vTHuEicMW8hxnC1UZVhrIaS3pyJAUZ2PSAthHtgMr22FV9KGSw9-PmrFI0APWdpYsroPTF1oBRDULy98krrsc_N9ygE5GJD8lIMIrFvEbquDEE4E6qK1gKpk4HZRfNY6sCcxIehnXZT7CxBQ2HqIhhrHN8KQEWXuxxUmZK0BtFS8a3z7I4GscFjEM0fMrKe9aoyiTxF709RL4mb-w0wbTHHLzcR1it6PAU4EorC6niNurNLJcfDWuPRJVaS8HyB_kXxUSRNbiHRQn_5VE8W3-OpxefbSQjax3QR_I-9oBg22iNcwou68pcsYGsKX5H07oXdTLq0YRZIyqOpa0G5vMbA19Sb925uSpGa5FpCpfnoumSOdeeVkJGBKcuJ0xPOObeX8VdpzuTZ6N_jyM5nxCBkfba0bwOXdRco75SpWfQ7CovQIgz6J2EjkUInSsO2FIP_wOLQoNMU2OnQ5vnyEchexodw824svrM8je_fXS0_kE1W2djk9jc8ZNzQPe77N3LALnqspaaq_87vZWL4w9Vc3gwDCLx4KNh2CqZy8UI93okSne1K111ZpydMw2WDtPMOC0XiNC7icQ%3D%3D)
---
Dear Friend,
---
The next few days may be the longest of my life. They will decide whether I live free or become a political prisoner.
---
Friday, November 21 is the **_DEADLINE_** for the Democrat Attorney General of Arizona to decide whether the _TRIAL OF MY LIFE_ proceeds or is thrown out once and for all.
---
It will truly be the _“trial of my life.”_ If I lose, I could be SENTENCED to 20 YEARS in prison as an innocent man.
---
And because this is a _state_ case, President Trump **_CANNOT_** save me with a _federal_ pardon.
---
But that didn’t stop the President from sending a POWERFUL and SYMBOLIC message to the Attorney General at the final hour.
---
**He granted me a pardon to show the Democrat Attorney General that the President of the United States and the Department of Justice recognize my innocence and stand beside me in the fight for my freedom.**
---
_Now, I am humbly asking _YOU_ to deliver a symbolic message at the final hour as well..._
---
_...I am asking you to sign _THE PEOPLE’S PARDON of Rudy W. Giuliani_ to show just how many U.S. Citizens believe that this RIGGED trial must be thrown to the ash heap of history._
---
_With the November 21 Deadline fast approaching, I’m asking**YOU** to echo the President’s call for my freedom!_
---
